front: Musical: Agnes de Mille choreographed this musical's dream ballet in which Laurey 'makes up her mind' about Jud and Curly
work: Dream Ballet ("Laurey Makes Up Her Mind")

front: Musical: this Rodgers and Hammerstein musical is credited with popularizing dream ballets in Broadway (though it did not invent them)
work: Dream Ballet ("Laurey Makes Up Her Mind")

front: Musical: the dream ballet in this musical begins after a character drinks the Elixir of Egypt and features the song 'Out of My Dreams'
work: Dream Ballet ("Laurey Makes Up Her Mind")

front: Musical: in the 2019 revival of this musical, John Heginbotham choreographed the dream sequence with performers dancing barefoot in a dark setting
work: Dream Ballet ("Laurey Makes Up Her Mind")

front: Song: 'chicks and ducks and geese better scurry' before a character describes the imagined vehicle
back: "The Surrey with the Fringe on Top" (Oklahoma!)

front: Song: this opening number of Oklahoma! has Curly proclaiming 'I've got a beautiful feeling / everything's going my way'
Song: this song's melody features prominently in Oklahoma!'s dream ballet
Musical: Daniel Fish's 2019 revival of this musical served cornbread and chili to audience members during intermission
Musical: in the 2019 revival, Ali Stroker played Ado Annie and became the first wheelchair-using performer to win a Tony
Song: in this Jud Fry solo, he imagines 'long tangled hair' which 'falls across my face' while vowing to win a bride
Musical: Rodgers and Hammerstein hired Agnes de Mille after watching her receive 22 curtain calls at the premiere of her ballet Rodeo
Musical: the original orchestration of this musical was by Robert Russell Bennett; John Wilson's 2023 recording was the first complete recording of it
Musical: this was Richard Rodgers' first musical after switching lyricists from Lorenz Hart to Oscar Hammerstein
